adr_preparation.PROPOSED_ADR_TITLE: Quote Preview PDF extraction result persistence and runtime ownership
adr_preparation.PROBLEM_STATEMENT: Forge has a probable canonical PDF preview engine and a confirmation modal, but no proven runtime owner, cache writer, cache reader or complete call chain connecting extraction output to Quote Preview UI.
DECISION_REQUIRED: Whether extraction output must persist beyond the current invocation.
Whether an existing generic repository or runtime state contract can be reused.
Which module owns the extraction-result lifecycle.
Which cache key or identity is authorized.
Which writer and reader APIs are canonical.
Which event or explicit handoff opens the confirmation modal.
How confirmed data remains preview-only and never becomes quote truth automatically.

OPTIONS_TO_EVALUATE.condition: Allowed only if a runtime owner, writer, reader, key and call chain are proven with source evidence.
Allowed only if persistence is not required and lifecycle, reload behavior and review safety are explicitly accepted.
Not authorized by this phase. Requires approved ADR, canonical ownership, schema, retention and deletion rules.

NON_NEGOTIABLE_GUARDRAILS: PDF engine output is reference data, not quote truth.
No duplicate cache, bridge, parser or engine.
No CRM, backend, policy, quote or provider writes.
No UI integration before the contract is approved.
